from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples about airports (or related entities) into a single
    fluent, complex sentence. Information belonging to the same subject is
    combined into one clause to avoid repetitive subject mentions.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    def _clean(val: str) -> str:
        """Remove surrounding quotes if present."""
        return val.strip('"').strip("'")

    def _format_number(value: str) -> str:
        """Strip trailing .0 from numeric strings and keep as is otherwise."""
        try:
            # Convert to float then int if no fractional part
            num = float(value)
            if num.is_integer():
                return str(int(num))
            return str(num)
        except ValueError:
            return value

    def _ordinal(prefix: str) -> str:
        """Return a lower‑cased ordinal string (e.g., '1st' -> 'first')."""
        mapping = {
            "1st": "first",
            "2nd": "second",
            "3rd": "third",
            "4th": "fourth",
            "5th": "fifth",
        }
        return mapping.get(prefix, prefix.lower())

    def _phrase(predicate: str, obj: str) -> str:
        """Return a predicate‑specific phrase without the subject."""
        obj = _format_number(obj)

        # Simple direct mappings
        simple_map = {
            "cityServed": lambda o: f"serves the city of {o}",
            "location": lambda o: f"is located in {o}",
            "country": lambda o: f"is in {o}",
            "isPartOf": lambda o: f"is part of {o}",
            "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel": lambda o: f"lies at an elevation of {o} metres above sea level",
            "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet": lambda o: f"lies at an elevation of {o} feet above sea level",
            "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres": lambda o: f"lies at an elevation of {o} metres above sea level",
            "operatingOrganisation": lambda o: f"is operated by {o}",
            "runwaySurfaceType": lambda o: f"has a runway surface of {o}",
            "runwayLength": lambda o: f"has a runway length of {o}",
            "runwayName": lambda o: f"has a runway named {o}",
            "locationIdentifier": lambda o: f"has location identifier {o}",
            "iataLocationIdentifier": lambda o: f"has IATA code {o}",
            "icaoLocationIdentifier": lambda o: f"has ICAO code {o}",
            "nativeName": lambda o: f"is also known as {o}",
            "owner": lambda o: f"is owned by {o}",
            "leader": lambda o: f"has leader {o}",
            "leaderTitle": lambda o: f"has leader title {o}",
            "leaderParty": lambda o: f"has leader from the {o}",
            "capital": lambda o: f"has capital {o}",
            "largestCity": lambda o: f"has largest city {o}",
            "mayor": lambda o: f"has mayor {o}",
            "language": lambda o: f"speaks {o}",
            "officialLanguage": lambda o: f"has official language {o}",
            "currency": lambda o: f"uses currency {o}",
            "headquarter": lambda o: f"has headquarters at {o}",
            "regionServed": lambda o: f"serves region {o}",
            "hubAirport": lambda o: f"has hub airport {o}",
            "aircraftHelicopter": lambda o: f"operates helicopter {o}",
            "transportAircraft": lambda o: f"operates transport aircraft {o}",
            "aircraftFighter": lambda o: f"operates fighter {o}",
            "attackAircraft": lambda o: f"operates attack aircraft {o}",
            "battle": lambda o: f"participated in {o}",
            "postalCode": lambda o: f"has postal code {o}",
            "areaCode": lambda o: f"has area code {o}",
            "foundingYear": lambda o: f"was founded in {o}",
            "foundedBy": lambda o: f"was founded by {o}",
            "chief": lambda o: f"has chief {o}",
            "jurisdiction": lambda o: f"is under jurisdiction of {o}",
            "demonym": lambda o: f"its demonym is {o}",
            "administrativeArrondissement": lambda o: f"is part of {o}",
            "city": lambda o: f"is associated with city {o}",
        }
        if predicate in simple_map:
            return simple_map[predicate](obj)

        # Indexed runway predicates
        if predicate.endswith("RunwayLengthFeet"):
            idx = predicate.split("Runway")[0]
            return f"has a {_ordinal(idx)} runway that is {obj} feet long"
        if predicate.endswith("RunwayLengthMetre"):
            idx = predicate.split("Runway")[0]
            return f"has a {_ordinal(idx)} runway that is {obj} metres long"
        if predicate.endswith("RunwaySurfaceType"):
            idx = predicate.split("Runway")[0]
            return f"has a {_ordinal(idx)} runway surface of {obj}"
        if predicate.endswith("RunwayNumber"):
            idx = predicate.split("Runway")[0]
            return f"has a {_ordinal(idx)} runway numbered {obj}"
        # Fallback for any other predicate – skip unknown predicates
        return None

    # Group phrases by subject
    subject_phrases: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
    for t in triples:
        subj = t.subject
        pred = t.predicate
        obj = _clean(t.object)
        phrase = _phrase(pred, obj)
        if phrase:  # skip unknown predicates
            subject_phrases.setdefault(subj, []).append(phrase)

    # Merge related subjects (e.g., owner or leader relationships) into relative clauses
    merged_subjects = set()
    clauses: list[str] = []

    # First pass: create clauses, merging where possible
    for subj, phrases in list(subject_phrases.items()):
        if not phrases or subj in merged_subjects:
            continue

        new_phrases: list[str] = []
        for phrase in phrases:
            # Ownership merging: "is owned by X" where X has its own phrases
            if phrase.startswith("is owned by "):
                obj = phrase[len("is owned by "):]
                if obj in subject_phrases and subject_phrases[obj]:
                    obj_phrases = subject_phrases[obj]
                    rel = f"is owned by {obj}, whose " + ", ".join(obj_phrases)
                    new_phrases.append(rel)
                    merged_subjects.add(obj)  # suppress separate clause for obj
                    continue

            # Leader merging: "has leader X" where X has its own phrases
            if phrase.startswith("has leader "):
                obj = phrase[len("has leader "):]
                if obj in subject_phrases and subject_phrases[obj]:
                    obj_phrases = subject_phrases[obj]
                    rel = f"has leader {obj}, who " + ", ".join(obj_phrases)
                    new_phrases.append(rel)
                    merged_subjects.add(obj)
                    continue

            # Location merging: "is located in X" where X has its own phrases
            if phrase.startswith("is located in "):
                obj = phrase[len("is located in "):]
                if obj in subject_phrases and subject_phrases[obj]:
                    obj_phrases = subject_phrases[obj]
                    rel = f"is located in {obj}, which " + ", ".join(obj_phrases)
                    new_phrases.append(rel)
                    merged_subjects.add(obj)
                    continue

            # Country merging: "is in X" where X has its own phrases
            if phrase.startswith("is in "):
                obj = phrase[len("is in "):]
                if obj in subject_phrases and subject_phrases[obj]:
                    obj_phrases = subject_phrases[obj]
                    rel = f"is in {obj}, which " + ", ".join(obj_phrases)
                    new_phrases.append(rel)
                    merged_subjects.add(obj)
                    continue

            new_phrases.append(phrase)

        if not new_phrases:
            continue

        if len(new_phrases) == 1:
            clause = f"{subj} {new_phrases[0]}"
        else:
            clause = f"{subj} " + ", ".join(new_phrases[:-1]) + ", and " + new_phrases[-1]
        clauses.append(clause)

    # Second pass: add any remaining subjects that were not merged or already processed
    processed_subjects = set()
    # First pass already added clauses for subjects; record them
    for clause in clauses:
        # extract subject (text before first space)
        subj = clause.split(' ', 1)[0]
        processed_subjects.add(subj)

    for subj, phrases in subject_phrases.items():
        if subj in merged_subjects or subj in processed_subjects or not phrases:
            continue
        if len(phrases) == 1:
            clause = f"{subj} {phrases[0]}"
        else:
            clause = f"{subj} " + ", ".join(phrases[:-1]) + ", and " + phrases[-1]
        clauses.append(clause)

    # Combine all clauses into a single sentence
    sentence = ", ".join(clauses) + "."
    # Capitalise first character
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END

                if obj in subject_phrases and subject_phrases[obj]:
                    obj_phrases = subject_phrases[obj]
                    rel = f"has leader {obj}, who " + ", ".join(obj_phrases)
                    new_phrases.append(rel)
                    merged_subjects.add(obj)
                    continue

            new_phrases.append(phrase)

        if not new_phrases:
            continue

        if len(new_phrases) == 1:
            clause = f"{subj} {new_phrases[0]}"
        else:
            clause = f"{subj} " + ", ".join(new_phrases[:-1]) + ", and " + new_phrases[-1]
        clauses.append(clause)

    # Second pass: add any remaining subjects that were not merged or already processed
    processed_subjects = set()
    # First pass already added clauses for subjects; record them
    for clause in clauses:
        # extract subject (text before first space)
        subj = clause.split(' ', 1)[0]
        processed_subjects.add(subj)

    for subj, phrases in subject_phrases.items():
        if subj in merged_subjects or subj in processed_subjects or not phrases:
            continue
        if len(phrases) == 1:
            clause = f"{subj} {phrases[0]}"
        else:
            clause = f"{subj} " + ", ".join(phrases[:-1]) + ", and " + phrases[-1]
        clauses.append(clause)

    # Combine all clauses into a single sentence
    sentence = ", ".join(clauses) + "."
    # Capitalise first character
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
```
